name: Chiapas Sandals — Dark Magenta
description: Handmade sandals from Chiapas with Eclectic Array's signature woven strap and a light, cork-like insole.
story: Each strap takes up to 8 hours to weave by hand
Fair-trade; each purchase supports artisan communities
objections: “I saw similar ones at the market for $40”
“What if they get wet?” — they shouldn't get wet; answer honestly

name: Threaded Huichol Skull
description: A statement art piece threaded by Huichol (Wixárika) artisans of the Sierra Madre Occidental; every symbol represents their culture and the nature around them.
story: Skulls are found in the mountains — no animal is harmed
Beeswax is applied to fix the thread
One piece takes about a month to complete
objections: “Is this a real skull? Was an animal hurt?”
“$1,624 is a lot for a decoration”
“How would I even ship this home safely?”

name: Tribal Macrame Earrings
description: Light, hand-threaded macramé earrings — easy to combine, one of a kind.
story: Hand-threaded by Joanna, a Venezuelan artisan living in Los Cabos
Her collection supports her family back in Venezuela
Designs vary slightly — each pair is unique
objections: “Do you have two exactly alike?” — no; each pair is unique, turn variation into value
